title: Decimals on the number line: tenths 0-1
description: Graph tenths between 0 and 1 on the number line.
topic_path: Decimals on the number line: tenths 0-1

assessment_items.question_content:
**Move the dot to $0.0$ on the number line.**

[[☃ number-line 1]]
hints: The number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces between $0$ and $1$.

Each tick mark represents a length of $\maroonD{0.1}$, or $\maroonD{\text{one tenth}}$.

![A number line from 0 to 1, labeled in steps of 1 tenth as decimals. A fraction bar the same length of the number line shows 10 equal sections, each labeled 1 tenth as a fraction.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b839436a00faf7a7695e7198da41314d3751a1cf)

Since $0.0=0$, we keep the dot at $\greenD{0}$.

![The number line with the point at 0](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9d1dad1ecab6fb305f0439aedd6e61cc50531d4b)

The answer

[[☃ number-line 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Move the dot to $3$ tenths on the number line.**

[[☃ number-line 1]]
hints: The number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces between $0$ and $1$.

Each tick mark represents a length of $\purpleC{0.1}$, or $\purpleC{\text{one tenth}}$.

![A number line from 0 to 1, labeled in steps of 1 tenth as decimals. A fraction bar the same length of the number line shows 10 equal sections, each labeled 1 tenth as a fraction.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b839436a00faf7a7695e7198da41314d3751a1cf)

Starting at $0$, we move a length of $\purpleC{0.1}$ three times to get to $\greenD{3\text{ tenths}}$.

![The fraction bar shows 3 of the 10 sections shaded and is labeled 3 tenths. The number line shows arrows from 0 to 3 tenths. Both figures model the same length.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/63c0931302902668a0e106179a15812b1ea71d0d)

The answer

[[☃ number-line 1]]

[[☃ number-line 1]]

Starting at $0$, we move a length of $\purpleD{0.1}$ six times to get to $\greenD{0.6}$.

![The fraction bar shows 6 of the 10 sections shaded and is labeled 6 tenths. The number line shows arrows from 0 to 6 tenths. Both figures model the same length.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5fa47e4568757676268bbd29b43a04ed5ffd7da1)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Move the dot to $9$ tenths on the number line.**

[[☃ number-line 1]]
hints: The number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces between $0$ and $1$.

Each tick mark represents a length of $\purpleC{0.1}$, or $\purpleC{\text{one tenth}}$.

![A number line from 0 to 1, labeled in steps of 1 tenth as decimals. A fraction bar the same length of the number line shows 10 equal sections, each labeled 1 tenth as a fraction.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b839436a00faf7a7695e7198da41314d3751a1cf)

Starting at $0$, we move a length of $\purpleC{0.1}$ nine times to get to $\greenD{9\,\text{tenths}}$.

![The fraction bar shows 9 of the 10 sections shaded and is labeled 9 tenths. The number line shows arrows from 0 to 9 tenths. Both figures model the same length.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f1e3f86940ab8abd77db3ca0fce6eeb50389530b)

[[☃ number-line 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Move the dot to $1.0$ on the number line.**

[[☃ number-line 1]]
hints: The number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces between $0$ and $1$.

Each tick mark represents a length of $\purpleC{0.1}$, or $\purpleC{\text{one tenth}}$.

![A number line from 0 to 1, labeled in steps of 1 tenth as decimals. A fraction bar the same length of the number line shows 10 equal sections, each labeled 1 tenth as a fraction.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b839436a00faf7a7695e7198da41314d3751a1cf)

Starting at $0$, we move a length of $\purpleC{0.1}$ ten times to get to $\greenD{1.0}$ or *one whole*.

![The fraction bar shows 10 of the 10 sections shaded and is labeled 10 tenths. The number line shows arrows from 0 to 10 tenths, which is the same position as 1. Both figures model the same length.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d7a48e64ce62040cbf842a24247763a8f676aef8)

The answer

[[☃ number-line 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the decimal value of the point on the number line?**

![A number line from 0 to 1, split in 10 equal sections. The point is 1 section to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/65c9b37d4af66898377e7f1c444ee4720b775c01)

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: The number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces between $0$ and $1$.

Each tick mark represents a length of $\maroonD{0.1}$, or $\maroonD{\text{one tenth}}$.

![A number line from 0 to 1, labeled in steps of 1 tenth as decimals. A fraction bar the same length of the number line shows 10 equal sections, each labeled 1 tenth as a fraction.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b839436a00faf7a7695e7198da41314d3751a1cf)

The point is $\blueD{ \text{1 tenth}}$ after $0$.

![The fraction bar shows 1 of the 10 sections shaded and is labeled 1 tenth. The number line shows arrows from 0 to 1 tenths. Both figures model the same length.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fa6036339986db9123a29f6d99264d5cd22eec43)
The decimal value of the point is $\blueD{0.1}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many tenths does the point on the number line represent?**

![A number line from 0 to 1, split in 10 equal sections. The point is 4 sections to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/58a8d0f320169ad9638371b21148baeac9504f5b)

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] tenths
hints: The number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces between $0$ and $1$.

Each tick mark represents a length of $\maroonD{0.1}$, or $\maroonD{\text{one tenth}}$.

![A number line from 0 to 1, labeled in steps of 1 tenth as decimals. A fraction bar the same length of the number line shows 10 equal sections, each labeled 1 tenth as a fraction.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b839436a00faf7a7695e7198da41314d3751a1cf)

The point is $\blueD{ \text{4 tenths}}$ after $0$.

![The fraction bar shows 4 of the 10 sections shaded and is labeled 3 tenths. The number line shows arrows from 0 to 4 tenths. Both figures model the same length.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7665a9cb7f85d6a4486d536775822ff1ef346a63)
The point on the number line represents $\blueD{4\,\text{tenths}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following number lines graphs the point at $2$ tenths?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces between $0$ and $1$.

Each tick mark represents a length of $\maroonD{0.1}$, or $\maroonD{\text{one tenth}}$.

![A number line from 0 to 1, labeled in steps of 1 tenth as decimals. A fraction bar the same length of the number line shows 10 equal sections, each labeled 1 tenth as a fraction.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b839436a00faf7a7695e7198da41314d3751a1cf)

The number $\blueD{2 \text{ tenths}}$ after $0$ is the same as $\blueD{0.2}$.

![The fraction bar shows 2 of the 10 sections shaded and is labeled 2 tenths. The number line shows arrows from 0 to 2 tenths. Both figures model the same length.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/27850ee87a60bb47375b492501135ab4ffbec39c)

The following number line graphs the point at $2$ tenths:

![The number line with the point 2 sections to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9deb36a2471eb1d8c7927c5833d854bba847f15e)
